title: Add configurable custom prompt support to unit-test plugin

subtasks.content: Add `customPrompt` setting (type `string`, default `""`, textarea) and `promptMode` setting (type `string`, default `"append"`, enum `["append", "replace"]`) to `.formic/plugins/unit-test/manifest.json` under the existing `settings` object

Extend `loadFormicServices()` in `.formic/plugins/unit-test/server.js` to also dynamically import `configStore.js` (alongside `pipelineRegistry.js` and `skillReader.js`) and return it as part of the services object

Add a `resolvePrompt(configStore)` async helper function in `server.js` that: (1) reads `customPrompt` via `configStore.getPluginSetting('unit-test', 'customPrompt')`, (2) reads `promptMode` via `configStore.getPluginSetting('unit-test', 'promptMode')`, (3) returns the built-in `SKILL_CONTENT` when `customPrompt` is empty/undefined, (4) returns `SKILL_CONTENT + '\n\n' + customPrompt` for `append` mode, (5) returns only `customPrompt` for `replace` mode

Update the `unitTestPlugin(fastify)` function in `server.js` to call `resolvePrompt(configStore)` and pass the resolved content to `skill.registerSkillOverride('unit-test', resolvedContent, 'unit-test')` instead of the hardcoded `SKILL_CONTENT`

Verify edge case handling: (a) empty string `customPrompt` with `append` mode → uses default, (b) empty string `customPrompt` with `replace` mode → uses default, (c) non-empty `customPrompt` with `append` → concatenates, (d) non-empty `customPrompt` with `replace` → uses only custom, (e) undefined/missing settings → falls back to defaults

Ensure template variables `$TASK_TITLE`, `$TASK_ID`, and `$TASK_DOCS_PATH` are preserved in all output paths (they are resolved at runtime by the workflow engine, so the plugin must not pre-resolve them)
Add inline JSDoc comment on `resolvePrompt()` documenting the merge logic and supported template variables for future maintainers
Run `npm run build` and verify no TypeScript or lint errors are introduced by the changes
Manually verify the Settings UI renders `customPrompt` as a textarea and `promptMode` as a dropdown with `append` and `replace` options when the unit-test plugin settings panel is opened
